### **Slide 1: Partnership to Platform (Title Slide)**

![][image1]  
**Your Context:** Introduction slide \- will likely use this time to introduce myself and my portfolio.

**Recommended Script:**  
"Good morning, everyone. It's a real honor to be here at the 10th Annual Accessibility Summit. The theme for today, 'A Decade of People & Technology,' is the perfect way to frame this.  
My name is \[Your Name\], and I lead the Verizon Design System, or VDS. My team and I live at the intersection of 'People' and 'Technology' every single day.

For the past several years, we've been on a journey. It's a journey that has taken us from a 'People'-first partnership to building a truly intelligent 'Platform.' Today, I want to share that story with you."

### **Slide 2: What is VDS? our "people partnership"**

![][image2]  
**Your Context:** Answer the question "what is VDS?" Ask how VDS is connected to adopters and stakeholders. Why is it our "people partnership?"

**Recommended Script:**  
"So, to get us on the same page, what is VDS?  
Sure, it's a collection of tools and code... but that's not what it *is*. At its core, VDS is a 'people partnership.'

It's the living, breathing agreement between all of our designers, engineers, brand managers, and accessibility experts. It's the 'village' that it takes to build accessible products at the scale of Verizon. VDS is simply a 'Technology' that enables our 'People' to work together."

### 

### **Slide 3: (VDS Parts: Fundamentals, Components, Docs)**

![][image3]  
**Your Context:** This slide leads into the specifics of what VDS is.

**Recommended Script:**  
"And what does that 'village' produce?  
It produces our Fundamentals: the foundational, accessible-by-design rules for brand, color, and typography.  
It produces our Components: the reusable, fully-vetted UI patterns that teams use to build.  
And most importantly, it produces our Documentation: a single source of truth for all our usage guides, design principles, and accessibility standards."

### 

### **Slide 4: The Value of VDS**

![][image4]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"When we apply this system, the value is immediate. It gives us consistency, quality, scalability, efficiency, and speed-to-market.  
But all of these are built on the most important value of all... Accessibility."

### 

### **Slide 5: Accessibility (Value Prop)**

![][image5]  
**Your Context:** Zooming in on accessibility specifically to read out the text on the slide.

**Recommended Script:**  
"VDS incorporates accessibility best practices at its core, making it easier to ensure that our products and services meet the needs of our diverse customer groups.  
Let's be honest: accessibility is *hard* to get right, every single time, on every product. VDS is a 'Technology' that can do much of that heavy lifting, scaling the expertise of our 'People' so our teams have a much stronger chance of getting it right by default."

### 

### **Slide 6: A New Status Quo (and the challenge of scale)**

![][image6]  
**Your Context:** Pivoting to how the design system is made and maintained. What does this slide mean? I need to define what this setup means.

**Recommended Script:**  
"So, that's our 'New Way,' right? We have this 'people partnership' (VDS) that gives us 'New Way' value (built-in accessibility).  
This is a huge win. And for a long time, this *was* our 'New Way.'

But this success created a *new* problem. It created a *new* Status Quo so-to-speak. And that is the challenge of scale.

Let me show you what I mean by looking at VDS’s own process..."   
(Team don’t hate on me if I didn’t get this exactly right)

### 

### **Slide 7: VDS Product Design Process**

![][image7]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This is our standard product design process. Nothing groundbreaking: Discover, Define, Design, Spec, Code, and Test.  
And in the beginning, our 'people partnership' with accessibility looked like this..."

### 

### **Slide 8: Process (Test at the end)**

![][image8]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This was our 'Status Quo 1.' We'd do all the work, and then, at the very end, we'd throw it over the wall to the accessibility team for testing.  
This was reactive. It was slow. It was inefficient. And it treated our accessibility partners like a compliance gate, not the co-creators they are."

### 

### **Slide 9: Process (Shift Left)**

![][image9]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"So, we created 'New Way 1.' We shifted left.  
Our 'people partnership' became our process. We stopped testing at the end and started engaging the Accessibility team at every critical stage: in ideation, in design, in isolated component development, and *again* in integration testing.

This is our 'people partnership' in action. It's fantastic. It ensures everything we build is born accessible.

But... this is where we hit our new problem."

### 

### **Slide 10: However, this has created a new problem**

![][image10]  
**Your Context:** This process works well in isolation... But what about when we have an update that needs to be applied to all components?

**Recommended Script:**  
"This process is perfect for building one component.  
But what happens when we need to update our color palette... and test it against *all* of our components?

What happens when we need to check *every* component for a new WCAG rule?

The number of human engagements... the number of meetings, reviews, and sign-offs... increases exponentially.

All of these engagements take time. People take time to meet, to communicate, to understand."

### 

### **Slide 11: ...people don't scale too well**

![][image11]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"And the truth is... people don't scale too well."

### 

### **Slide 12: An Intelligent Platform**

![][image12]  
**Your Context:** Introduce automation/AI... This should have a more specific connection to Accessibility.

**Recommended Script:**  
"This led us to begin to think about another 'New Way.'  
If our 'People' hit a wall at scale, our 'Technology' has to get smarter. We realized we need to build **an Intelligent Platform.**

We need to take the expertise of our 'people partnership'—all those rules, all that knowledge from our Accessibility experts—and embed it directly *into* the platform itself.

This is how we can scale our partnership. By automating it."

### 

### **Slide 13: AI Integration Opportunities**

![][image13]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"So, our team began mapping our process again. But this time, we looked at every single manual, human engagement point—especially our accessibility checks—and we reframed them.  
They are no longer just meetings; they are **AI Integration Opportunities**."

### 

### **Slide 14: AI Opportunities (Labeled)**

![][image14]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"And these opportunities are everywhere.  
We saw an opportunity for Context Aggregation in the Define stage.  
We saw an opportunity for Paired Design and Paired Programming in the Create stages.  
And we saw a massive opportunity for VQA and Testing in the Test stage."

### 

### **Slide 15: AI driven context aggregation**

![][image15]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Let's start with Context Aggregation. This is the foundational 'brain' of our system.  
We are teaching AI by unifying all of our scattered knowledge: our component documentation, our specs, our code... *and* our WCAG 2.1 AA requirements.

But we're also looking to enrich it with *human* data such as our user research, our market insights, and our live analytics.

This is transforming our design system from a static library into a dynamic 'brain' that understands the *why* behind our rules. Our accessibility guidelines are no longer a separate checklist; they are a core, contextualized data source woven into the very fabric of the system."

### 

### **Slide 16: (VDS Brain)**

![][image16]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"We asked ourselves a simple question:  
'Can we take our entire Verizon Design System... all of our guidelines, our documentation, our spec files, and our proprietary code... and use it to teach an AI to be our perfect, on-brand, intelligent partner?'

A partner that knows *everything* about VDS."

### 

### **Slide 17: (Empathetic Brain)**

![][image17]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"But then can we take it further And ask a deeper question:  
'Can we take that same partner... and layer in the context of our audience? All of our user research, our analytics data, and our accessibility guidelines... to make it a truly *empathetic*, inclusive, and data-driven partner?'"

### 

### **Slide 18: (The Full Brain)**

![][image18]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This is the intelligent core we are actively building. A platform that reflects not just our code, but our partnerships, our user insights, and our core accessibility principles."

### 

### **Slide 19: AI driven paired... creating**

![][image19]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Once we have the 'brain,' we can move towards AI-Driven Paired Creating.  
This is the 'proactive co-pilot' that brings that intelligence directly into our team's workflow, right inside Figma and their code editors.

The impact on accessibility is transformative. We move from *reactive auditing and checks* to *proactive compliance*.

The AI can instantly flag a low-contrast color choice. It can suggest the correct ARIA labels *as you code*. And, as we'll see, it can warn you if a design conflicts with a known accessibility rule."

### 

### **Slide 20: (Brain \-\> Figma / React)**

![][image20]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This 'brain' becomes a co-pilot for our designers in Figma, and a co-pilot for our engineers in their IDE, both powered by the same, single source of truth."

### 

### **Slide 21: AI driven testing**

![][image21]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Finally, we're building AI-Driven Testing.  
This can act as a 'comprehensive safety net' that guarantees our standards are met at a scale that people *cannot*.

Because the AI is trained on our *specific* WCAG requirements and our component specs, it can automatically generate and run thousands of test cases—covering every edge case, screen reader flow, and keyboard interaction.

This automates the most time-consuming part of quality assurance and ensures our accessibility commitments are not just *defined*, but *enforced*."

### 

### **Slide 22: (VQA Examples)**

![][image22]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Let me show you two quick examples. This is our Component VQA (Visual Quality Assurance) tool.  
This tool can assist VQA by automating it. It can scan and compare a Figma file to a coded component and show *only* the differences. Here, it's catching a font size error... here, a padding error... and here, it's passing a perfect match.

### 

### **Slide 23: Variant testing can get a little crazy**

![][image23]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Then there's variant testing. This is what it looks like to test one component.  
For a single complex component, creating the test case examples can take a designer **4 to 6 hours**.

It's a high-effort, repetitive, manual task. And it's the *perfect* candidate for AI automation. We're building a tool that generates these test cases in seconds, not hours."

### 

### **Slide 24: The Next Steps (a three pillar framework)**

![][image24]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"So, this is our new 'New Way.' An intelligent platform that scales our 'people partnership.'  
This is our vision. But a vision is nothing without a plan.

Our 'Next Steps' are built on a simple, three-pillar framework."

### 

### **Slide 25: Where we go from here (3 Pillars)**

![][image25]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This is the 'how.'  
**First, we Empower.** We want to give our teams the tools they need to be successful.

**Second, we Train.** A tool is useless if no one knows how to use it. We're investing in teaching new skills, like prompt engineering, to make our teams *partners* with AI.

**And third, we Aggregate.** This is the most important part. We are centralizing and structuring our context—our proprietary data, our design rules, our accessibility guidelines—so the AI can give *Verizon-specific* guidance at *exactly* the right time.

### 

### **Slide 26: The Next Decade (people & technology)**

![][image26]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"This framework is our plan for the next decade of 'People & Technology.'  
We are building an intelligent platform not to *replace* our people, but to *elevate* them.

We automate the platform... to scale our partnership... to free our people to solve the next generation of complex, human-centered accessibility challenges.

This is the future of 'People & Technology' at Verizon."

### 

### **Slide 27: (Verizon logo)**

![][image27]  
**Recommended Script:**  
"Thank you."
